marketState: bullish tilt — extreme cooling demand and a crowded managed-money short offset by a bearish storage surplus
summary: Weather is the standout signal: both the 1-7D and 8-16D demand windows are running +27.4%/+21.1% above the 5-year norm with rangePos above 1.0, i.e. forecast demand exceeds the entire historical range, though the 24h change (-7.3) shows the latest reading has ticked down slightly. Seasonality is mildly constructive (price already +2.2pp above the typical year, 63% odds of being higher in 4 weeks, +2.3% typical 4-week move) but the 51pp 10-90% spread makes it a low-reliability signal this week. Front price ($3.206) sits inside the fair-value band ($2.429-$3.429, fair $2.929) at 'SLIGHTLY OVER' now (+$0.277), but that premium shrinks to 'FAIR' by +14D/+21D as the fair-value band itself rises to meet price — the market is not badly stretched. Managed money is net short at only the 18th percentile of the 5-year range (contrarian bullish, fuel for short covering into heat), while storage is a genuine headwind at +175 Bcf vs 5y with a bigger-than-forecast build (+13 Bcf bearish surprise), and daily/weekly technicals show a bearish trend structure (50<200 EMA on 1d/1w). Net read: near-term heat-driven demand and stretched short positioning argue for upside risk that storage bears and daily-chart trend followers are underpricing, but the surplus caps how far a rally can run without a supportive storage print, and intraday market-data feeds failed in this snapshot so short-timeframe technicals carry extra uncertainty.

longTerm.entry: $3.00-$3.15 (scale in on dips toward fair value)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk (risk ≈ $0.20-0.30/MMBtu per contract), scale in over 2 tranches
longTerm.rationale: Both weather windows show cooling demand well above 5-year norms and above the historical max range, the strongest single input in this dataset, which typically pressures the current +175 Bcf storage surplus lower over the following weeks. Seasonality (63% odds higher in 4 weeks) and a contrarian-bullish COT setup (managed money net short at just the 18th percentile of 5 years) reinforce the case for upside over a multi-week horizon, tempered to medium conviction by low seasonality reliability and the bearish storage surprise.
longTerm.invalidation: A cooldown in the 8-16D demand forecast back toward/below the 5y norm, or a string of larger-than-forecast storage builds that push the deviation further above +175 Bcf vs 5y, would invalidate this view.

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term signals conflict: price is already slightly over fair value (+$0.277) and daily/weekly technical trend is bearish, while 1-7D weather demand is extreme (above range max) which normally supports the front month. Intraday market-data feeds (15m/1h/1w) failed to load in this snapshot, so short-timeframe technicals should be treated as low-confidence rather than acted on directly.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a pullback into the $2.95-$3.05 fair-value zone to buy, or a confirmed daily close below ~$3.10 with intraday data restored to consider a short; also watch whether the 24h weather-demand decline (-7.3) extends into a broader cooldown
